dependencies: Certain components and raw materials are available only from a sole supplier
Global shortages of certain components such as semiconductors and resins have previously caused disruptions
Several key products are manufactured or sterilized at particular facilities with constrained capacity and limited options for alternate sterilization facilities
risks: Reduction or interruption in supply from sole suppliers
Global shortages of semiconductors and resins
Stringent FDA regulations may prevent quickly establishing replacement sources
Commodity price volatility for resins, petroleum-based materials, and pulp
Natural disasters, pandemics, and geopolitical conflicts affecting supply chain
Regulatory requirements on safe use of chemicals including ethylene oxides and PFAS

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation by FDA and other government agencies worldwide
Medical Device Regulation (EU MDR) implementation with extended deadlines through 2028
Pillar Two Model Rules defining global minimum tax at 15% effective in fiscal year 2025
Data privacy and security laws including GDPR, HIPAA, HITECH, UK privacy laws, China PIPL
EU AI Act regulating artificial intelligence use
Potential workforce reductions and agency reorganization at US FDA impacting approval timelines
Changes in reimbursement policies by Medicare, Medicaid, and private payors
Physician Payments Sunshine Act (Open Payments) transparency reporting requirements
Environmental health and safety laws on hazardous materials and chemical constituents
Trade regulations including tariffs and export controls
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and anti-corruption laws globally
Economic sanctions by OFAC and export restrictions by BIS
Potential changes to US tax laws affecting foreign corporations
Denial of US government contracts to companies that redomicile abroad

sanction_risks: US economic sanctions on Iran, Syria, Cuba, Crimea, Russia, and Belarus
Limited business dealings in sanctioned countries expose to heightened risk of violating sanctions regulations
Russia-Ukraine conflict sanctions creating barriers to doing business in Russia and Belarus
Potential impact on global supply chains from sanctions including materials like palladium and neon dependent on Russia supply
OFAC and BIS restrictions on transactions with certain countries, governments, entities, and individuals
Potential penalties including civil fines, criminal sanctions, denial of export privileges, and debarment from government contracts
